rules.hint: test runs belong in a subagent so their output stays off the main thread
lint/typecheck runs belong in a subagent so their output stays off the main thread

this is an implementation write — most often the research→implementation handover, where the main thread (fresh off a subagent's findings) starts writing the code itself. Delegate the implementation to a subagent: it writes and self-verifies, you review the returned diff. If you just received a subagent's findings/results, the work that follows is the delegable unit, NOT a trivial exception — delegate it. Proceed inline only for an isolated one-line fix you did not just plan or research via a subagent

this is an implementation edit — most often the research→implementation handover, where the main thread (fresh off a subagent's findings) starts editing the code itself. Delegate the implementation to a subagent: it edits and self-verifies, you review the returned diff. If you just received a subagent's findings/results, the work that follows is the delegable unit, NOT a trivial exception — delegate it. Proceed inline only for an isolated one-line fix you did not just plan or research via a subagent
